understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate the player character to the goal gem tile marked '+' in each level. The player needed to reach this tile by moving horizontally and using gravity to fall into position, while avoiding spike tiles (u/v) that would end the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was horizontal only (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT), after which gravity automatically pulled the player in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid tile. In this level gravity was DOWN, meaning the player fell toward higher y values after each horizontal move. The corridor_scan and left_landing/right_landing previews showed where the player would end up after a move — passages indicated drops to different y-levels. CLICK actions removed breakable blocks (marked 'x') to open new fall paths, and importantly did NOT count as a movement turn. The level had a 50-turn budget per attempt and 2 attempts per level. Landing on spikes (u/v) immediately ended the attempt. UNDO could revert the previous action. The strategy involved clicking breakable blocks to create passages, then moving toward those passages to fall through to lower sections of the level toward the goal gem.
